title: Seismic reflectivity of the subducting Nazca plate and fluid-related processes at the north Chilean subduction zone
abstract: Seismic reflectivity of the subducting Nazca plate is studied in north Chile using passive seismic data from a combined onshore-offshore network. Intraslab reflectors and plate boundary reflectivity are correlated with fluid release processes along the subducting slab. High reflectivity zones indicate elevated pore fluid pressure at the megathrust and within the subducting oceanic crust.

location.description: North Chilean subduction zone ~19°–23°S; seismic reflectivity of subducting Nazca plate using passive network data

processing.summary: Passive seismic data from a combined onshore-offshore network in north Chile (~19°–23°S) are used to extract seismic reflectivity of the subducting Nazca plate. Intraslab reflectors and plate boundary reflectivity are analyzed in relation to fluid release processes. High reflectivity zones are interpreted as indicators of elevated pore fluid pressure.
